questions.prompt: Which condition in Weimar Germany during the early 1920s is most directly associated with the printing of vast quantities of currency to pay reparations and domestic debts?
choices: Hyperinflation that wiped out middle-class savings
Rapid industrial growth fueled by gold reserves
A balanced budget achieved through new tariffs
Population decline caused by famine
questions.correctText: Hyperinflation that wiped out middle-class savings

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Versailles contributed to political instability in Germany primarily because it
choices: restored the Hohenzollern monarchy
imposed harsh reparations and a war-guilt clause on Germany
granted Germany new colonies in Africa
permitted Germany to rearm without limits
questions.correctText: imposed harsh reparations and a war-guilt clause on Germany

questions.prompt: The collapse of stock prices on Wall Street in October 1929 had a global impact mainly because
choices: European economies depended on American loans and trade
the gold standard had already been abandoned worldwide
Japan replaced the United States as the world's largest economy
Latin American nations refused to trade with Europe
questions.correctText: European economies depended on American loans and trade

questions.prompt: Which development best illustrates the cultural changes of the "Roaring Twenties" in the West?
choices: The widespread rejection of jazz music and consumer goods
The expansion of mass media, automobile ownership, and new roles for women
The return to agricultural life in most Western cities
The end of women's suffrage movements in Europe
questions.correctText: The expansion of mass media, automobile ownership, and new roles for women

questions.prompt: Benito Mussolini came to power in Italy in 1922 largely by
choices: winning a free national election with a clear majority
leading the March on Rome and being appointed prime minister by the king
staging a popular revolution against the Catholic Church
receiving direct military support from Nazi Germany
questions.correctText: leading the March on Rome and being appointed prime minister by the king

questions.prompt: A central feature of fascism as practiced in interwar Italy and Germany was
choices: the protection of free speech and a multiparty system
extreme nationalism combined with one-party rule under a single leader
the public ownership of all private property
a commitment to international cooperation and disarmament
questions.correctText: extreme nationalism combined with one-party rule under a single leader

questions.prompt: In Mein Kampf, Adolf Hitler argued chiefly for
choices: German territorial expansion and the racial superiority of the so-called Aryans
European unity through a democratic federation
an alliance with the Soviet Union against Britain
the restoration of the Weimar constitution
questions.correctText: German territorial expansion and the racial superiority of the so-called Aryans

questions.prompt: The Reichstag fire of February 1933 was used by the Nazi regime to
choices: justify emergency decrees that suspended civil liberties in Germany
outlaw the Roman Catholic Church
force Hitler to step down from the chancellorship
negotiate a peace treaty with France
questions.correctText: justify emergency decrees that suspended civil liberties in Germany

questions.prompt: The Nuremberg Laws of 1935 contributed to the persecution of German Jews primarily by
choices: stripping them of citizenship and forbidding marriage with non-Jewish Germans
requiring them to serve in the German army
granting them autonomous regions in eastern Germany
creating special economic privileges for Jewish businesses
questions.correctText: stripping them of citizenship and forbidding marriage with non-Jewish Germans

questions.prompt: Kristallnacht, on the night of November 9–10, 1938, is best described as
choices: a coordinated wave of attacks on Jewish homes, businesses, and synagogues across Germany and Austria
an attempted military coup against Hitler led by army officers
a peaceful protest of Jewish citizens against Nazi rule
the day Germany formally annexed Czechoslovakia
questions.correctText: a coordinated wave of attacks on Jewish homes, businesses, and synagogues across Germany and Austria

questions.prompt: Totalitarian regimes in the 1930s commonly maintained power by
choices: allowing competing political parties to debate openly
using propaganda, secret police, and the suppression of dissent
rotating leadership through elections every two years
encouraging independent labor unions and a free press
questions.correctText: using propaganda, secret police, and the suppression of dissent

questions.prompt: The Spanish Civil War (1936–1939) is significant in interwar history because it
choices: showed the willingness of fascist powers to intervene militarily while democracies hesitated
ended in the restoration of the Spanish monarchy under a constitutional system
produced a long period of stable democracy in Spain
was concluded by the League of Nations through arbitration
questions.correctText: showed the willingness of fascist powers to intervene militarily while democracies hesitated

questions.prompt: General Francisco Franco emerged from the Spanish Civil War as
choices: the leader of an authoritarian regime that ruled Spain for decades
a constitutional monarch with limited powers
an exile in the Soviet Union
the prime minister of a parliamentary democracy
questions.correctText: the leader of an authoritarian regime that ruled Spain for decades

questions.prompt: Japan's invasion of Manchuria in 1931 reflected the rise of
choices: militarism and the desire for raw materials in Showa-era Japan
Western liberal democracy in East Asia
Japanese pacifism following World War I
a renewed commitment to the League of Nations
questions.correctText: militarism and the desire for raw materials in Showa-era Japan

questions.prompt: The Nanjing Massacre of 1937 is generally remembered as
choices: a mass atrocity committed by the Japanese Imperial Army against Chinese civilians and prisoners
a victory of the Chinese army over Japanese forces
a peaceful surrender of the Nationalist government to Japan
a Soviet-led offensive in northern China
questions.correctText: a mass atrocity committed by the Japanese Imperial Army against Chinese civilians and prisoners

questions.prompt: Japan's decision to attack Pearl Harbor in December 1941 was motivated chiefly by
choices: a desire to neutralize the United States Pacific Fleet while expanding into Southeast Asia
a long-standing alliance with Britain
fear of an invasion by Nazi Germany
the demands of the League of Nations
questions.correctText: a desire to neutralize the United States Pacific Fleet while expanding into Southeast Asia

questions.prompt: Joseph Stalin consolidated his control of the Soviet Union in the 1930s by
choices: using purges, show trials, and a vast secret police to eliminate rivals
creating a multiparty parliamentary system
restoring the Russian Orthodox Church as a state institution
withdrawing the USSR from all international affairs
questions.correctText: using purges, show trials, and a vast secret police to eliminate rivals

questions.prompt: The Munich Conference of 1938 is best remembered as an example of
choices: the policy of appeasement allowing Hitler to take the Sudetenland
successful collective security through the League of Nations
a peaceful resolution that prevented further German expansion
a Soviet-led effort to contain Nazi Germany
questions.correctText: the policy of appeasement allowing Hitler to take the Sudetenland

questions.prompt: When British Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain proclaimed "peace for our time" after Munich, he was expressing
choices: confidence that diplomacy with Hitler would prevent another general war
support for an immediate British declaration of war on Germany
a plan to ally Britain with the Soviet Union
his rejection of the Treaty of Versailles
questions.correctText: confidence that diplomacy with Hitler would prevent another general war

questions.prompt: The Nazi-Soviet Pact of August 1939 surprised the world primarily because it
choices: united two ideological enemies in a nonaggression agreement that included secret plans to divide Poland
ended the Spanish Civil War
created a formal military alliance between Germany and Britain
guaranteed the independence of all Eastern European states
questions.correctText: united two ideological enemies in a nonaggression agreement that included secret plans to divide Poland

questions.prompt: Mohandas Gandhi's Salt March of 1930 was significant because it
choices: dramatized civil disobedience against the British salt monopoly in India
ended British rule in India immediately
convinced Britain to abandon the partition of Bengal
sparked a violent uprising across the Indian subcontinent
questions.correctText: dramatized civil disobedience against the British salt monopoly in India

questions.prompt: Gandhi's method of satyagraha is best described as
choices: nonviolent resistance grounded in moral and spiritual conviction
armed rebellion supported by foreign powers
a parliamentary strategy of constitutional reform alone
passive acceptance of British colonial rule
questions.correctText: nonviolent resistance grounded in moral and spiritual conviction

questions.prompt: Which event is most often cited as the immediate beginning of World War II in Europe?
choices: Germany's invasion of Poland on September 1, 1939
Japan's attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941
Italy's invasion of Ethiopia in 1935
Germany's annexation of Austria in 1938
questions.correctText: Germany's invasion of Poland on September 1, 1939

questions.prompt: The German military strategy known as blitzkrieg relied chiefly on
choices: rapid, coordinated attacks by tanks, aircraft, and infantry
long sieges of fortified cities
trench warfare similar to that of World War I
naval blockades of enemy ports
questions.correctText: rapid, coordinated attacks by tanks, aircraft, and infantry

questions.prompt: The Fall of France in June 1940 was significant because it
choices: left Britain as the principal power resisting Nazi Germany in Western Europe
brought the United States immediately into the war
ended the Battle of the Atlantic
led to a German alliance with the Soviet Union
questions.correctText: left Britain as the principal power resisting Nazi Germany in Western Europe

questions.prompt: The Battle of Britain in 1940 was primarily
choices: an air campaign in which the Royal Air Force defended against the German Luftwaffe
a land invasion of southern England by German troops
a naval battle in the Mediterranean Sea
a series of bombings of Berlin by British forces
questions.correctText: an air campaign in which the Royal Air Force defended against the German Luftwaffe

questions.prompt: The evacuation at Dunkirk in 1940 is remembered chiefly for
choices: the rescue of hundreds of thousands of Allied soldiers from the French coast
the destruction of the German Wehrmacht
the surrender of Britain to Germany
the entry of the Soviet Union into the war
questions.correctText: the rescue of hundreds of thousands of Allied soldiers from the French coast

questions.prompt: Operation Barbarossa, launched in June 1941, refers to
choices: the German invasion of the Soviet Union
the Allied invasion of North Africa
the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor
the bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
questions.correctText: the German invasion of the Soviet Union

questions.prompt: The Lend-Lease Act of 1941 allowed the United States to
choices: supply Britain, the USSR, and other Allies with weapons and material while still officially neutral
send American combat troops into Europe immediately
purchase colonies in the Pacific from Britain
officially declare war on Germany
questions.correctText: supply Britain, the USSR, and other Allies with weapons and material while still officially neutral

questions.prompt: The Atlantic Charter, signed in 1941 by Franklin D. Roosevelt and Winston Churchill, outlined
choices: broad Allied war aims, including self-determination and free trade after the war
the formal entry of the United States into the war
the division of postwar Germany into zones
the use of atomic weapons against Japan
questions.correctText: broad Allied war aims, including self-determination and free trade after the war

questions.prompt: After the attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941, the United States
choices: entered World War II as an Allied power against Japan, Germany, and Italy
remained officially neutral until 1944
negotiated a peace treaty with Japan
joined the Axis powers
questions.correctText: entered World War II as an Allied power against Japan, Germany, and Italy

questions.prompt: The Battle of Midway in June 1942 is often described as a turning point because it
choices: crippled the Japanese carrier fleet and shifted the initiative in the Pacific to the United States
ended the war in the Pacific entirely
destroyed the entire United States Navy
led to the surrender of Germany
questions.correctText: crippled the Japanese carrier fleet and shifted the initiative in the Pacific to the United States

questions.prompt: The Allied strategy of "island-hopping" in the Pacific involved
choices: seizing key islands while bypassing heavily defended ones to move closer to Japan
invading mainland China before any island campaign
negotiating peace island by island with Japan
abandoning the Pacific to focus only on Europe
questions.correctText: seizing key islands while bypassing heavily defended ones to move closer to Japan

questions.prompt: The 1945 battles of Iwo Jima and Okinawa are remembered chiefly for
choices: their extremely high casualties and their role in bringing American forces close to Japan
ending the war in Europe
marking the first use of jet aircraft in combat
halting the Japanese advance into India
questions.correctText: their extremely high casualties and their role in bringing American forces close to Japan

questions.prompt: The Battle of Stalingrad (1942–1943) is widely regarded as a turning point because it
choices: destroyed a German army and began a sustained Soviet advance westward
ended the war in the Pacific
secured German control over the Caucasus oil fields
forced Britain out of the war
questions.correctText: destroyed a German army and began a sustained Soviet advance westward

questions.prompt: Allied campaigns in North Africa in 1942–1943 were important because they
choices: secured Mediterranean supply routes and opened a path to invade Italy
ended Japanese expansion in Southeast Asia
led directly to the surrender of Germany
brought the United States into the war
questions.correctText: secured Mediterranean supply routes and opened a path to invade Italy

questions.prompt: The Allied invasion of Italy in 1943 led to
choices: the fall of Mussolini and Italy's eventual switch to the Allied side
the immediate liberation of all of Italy
a Soviet occupation of Rome
the entry of Spain into the war
questions.correctText: the fall of Mussolini and Italy's eventual switch to the Allied side

questions.prompt: D-Day, June 6, 1944, refers to
choices: the Allied amphibious landings on the beaches of Normandy in France
the German surrender to Soviet forces in Berlin
the bombing of Tokyo by American planes
the signing of the Yalta agreements
questions.correctText: the Allied amphibious landings on the beaches of Normandy in France

questions.prompt: The Battle of the Bulge in late 1944 represented
choices: Germany's last major offensive on the Western Front
the first Allied landing in France
the Soviet capture of Stalingrad
the Japanese invasion of the Philippines
questions.correctText: Germany's last major offensive on the Western Front

questions.prompt: On the Eastern Front in 1944–1945, Soviet armies
choices: advanced across Eastern Europe and pushed toward Berlin
retreated to defend Moscow
joined Germany in attacking the Western Allies
signed a separate peace with Hitler
questions.correctText: advanced across Eastern Europe and pushed toward Berlin

questions.prompt: The Wannsee Conference of January 1942 is significant because it
choices: coordinated the implementation of the so-called Final Solution against European Jews
ended the German invasion of the Soviet Union
negotiated peace between Italy and the Allies
established the United Nations
questions.correctText: coordinated the implementation of the so-called Final Solution against European Jews

questions.prompt: The Nazi policy known as the "Final Solution" referred to
choices: the systematic mass murder of European Jews through ghettos, mobile killing units, and death camps
the resettlement of Jews in a Jewish homeland
the use of slave labor only
the deportation of Jews to neutral countries
questions.correctText: the systematic mass murder of European Jews through ghettos, mobile killing units, and death camps

questions.prompt: Auschwitz is most accurately described as
choices: the largest Nazi concentration and extermination camp complex in occupied Poland
a German labor camp that did not carry out mass killings
a refugee camp protected by the Red Cross
a Soviet prison camp in Siberia
questions.correctText: the largest Nazi concentration and extermination camp complex in occupied Poland

questions.prompt: The Holocaust is generally defined as the
choices: state-sponsored, systematic genocide of approximately six million Jews by Nazi Germany and its collaborators
internment of Japanese-Americans during World War II
Soviet famine in Ukraine in the 1930s
Allied bombing campaign against German cities
questions.correctText: state-sponsored, systematic genocide of approximately six million Jews by Nazi Germany and its collaborators

questions.prompt: In addition to Jewish victims, Nazi mass murder also targeted
choices: Roma, people with disabilities, political prisoners, and Soviet prisoners of war
only military prisoners of war
only German civilians who opposed Hitler
only citizens of neutral countries
questions.correctText: Roma, people with disabilities, political prisoners, and Soviet prisoners of war

questions.prompt: Genocide is most precisely defined as
choices: the deliberate destruction, in whole or in part, of a national, ethnic, racial, or religious group
any war that produces large civilian casualties
the forced movement of populations across borders
the bombing of cities during wartime
questions.correctText: the deliberate destruction, in whole or in part, of a national, ethnic, racial, or religious group

questions.prompt: The Yalta Conference of February 1945 brought together
choices: the Big Three—Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin—to plan the postwar order in Europe
the leaders of Germany, Italy, and Japan
Mahatma Gandhi and British colonial officials
the founding members of the League of Nations
questions.correctText: the Big Three—Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin—to plan the postwar order in Europe

questions.prompt: A major outcome of Yalta was that
choices: Germany would be divided into occupation zones administered by the Allied powers
the Soviet Union would withdraw from Eastern Europe immediately
atomic weapons would be banned worldwide
Japan would be split into American and Soviet halves
questions.correctText: Germany would be divided into occupation zones administered by the Allied powers

questions.prompt: Adolf Hitler's death on April 30, 1945 occurred
choices: by suicide in his Berlin bunker as Soviet forces closed in on the city
on the battlefield outside Stalingrad
in a plot by his own generals
as a war crimes defendant in Nuremberg
questions.correctText: by suicide in his Berlin bunker as Soviet forces closed in on the city

questions.prompt: V-E Day, May 8, 1945, marked
choices: the formal end of fighting in Europe following Germany's surrender
the end of the war in the Pacific
the day the United Nations was founded
the use of the first atomic bomb
questions.correctText: the formal end of fighting in Europe following Germany's surrender

questions.prompt: The Potsdam Conference in July 1945
choices: addressed the postwar treatment of Germany and issued an ultimatum demanding Japan's surrender
ended Allied cooperation immediately
produced the Treaty of Versailles
created the European Union
questions.correctText: addressed the postwar treatment of Germany and issued an ultimatum demanding Japan's surrender

questions.prompt: The atomic bombings of Hiroshima on August 6, 1945, and Nagasaki on August 9, 1945, were carried out by
choices: the United States, with the aim of forcing Japan to surrender
the Soviet Union, against Japanese forces in Manchuria
Britain, against Japanese-held islands
Nazi Germany, before its own surrender
questions.correctText: the United States, with the aim of forcing Japan to surrender

questions.prompt: V-J Day, August 15, 1945, marked
choices: Japan's announcement of surrender, effectively ending World War II
the start of the Korean War
the founding of the United Nations
the signing of the Atlantic Charter
questions.correctText: Japan's announcement of surrender, effectively ending World War II

questions.prompt: The Nuremberg Trials following World War II are most associated with
choices: holding leading Nazis legally accountable for war crimes and crimes against humanity
punishing only ordinary German soldiers
establishing the modern European Union
ending colonial rule in Asia
questions.correctText: holding leading Nazis legally accountable for war crimes and crimes against humanity

questions.prompt: The Tokyo war crimes trials after World War II
choices: prosecuted Japanese military and political leaders for crimes committed during the war
exonerated all Japanese officials from any wartime conduct
were conducted entirely by Japan without Allied involvement
led to the immediate restoration of the Japanese empire
questions.correctText: prosecuted Japanese military and political leaders for crimes committed during the war

questions.prompt: The United Nations, founded in 1945, was created chiefly to
choices: maintain international peace and promote cooperation among nations after World War II
guarantee the survival of European colonial empires
form an exclusive Western military alliance
replace national governments with a world parliament
questions.correctText: maintain international peace and promote cooperation among nations after World War II

questions.prompt: The atomic bombings of Japan in 1945 helped usher in the "atomic age" by
choices: demonstrating the destructive power of nuclear weapons and shaping postwar geopolitics
ending all research into nuclear technology
abolishing the use of conventional weapons
preventing the development of further nuclear arsenals
questions.correctText: demonstrating the destructive power of nuclear weapons and shaping postwar geopolitics

questions.prompt: Tensions between the United States and the Soviet Union at the end of World War II contributed to
choices: the emergence of the Cold War, marked by ideological rivalry and an arms race
an immediate joint occupation of all former Axis powers
the dissolution of both nations' militaries
the unification of Germany into a single democratic state in 1945
questions.correctText: the emergence of the Cold War, marked by ideological rivalry and an arms race

questions.prompt: The internment of Japanese-Americans during World War II involved
choices: the forced relocation of people of Japanese ancestry on the U.S. West Coast to camps
the voluntary migration of Japanese citizens to Hawaii
the deportation of all Japanese-Americans to Japan
the integration of Japanese-Americans into elite military units only
questions.correctText: the forced relocation of people of Japanese ancestry on the U.S. West Coast to camps

questions.prompt: The image of "Rosie the Riveter" symbolized
choices: American women taking on industrial and wartime jobs traditionally held by men
the rationing of food and gasoline at home
the role of female combat troops in Europe
the suffrage movement of the 1920s
questions.correctText: American women taking on industrial and wartime jobs traditionally held by men

questions.prompt: During World War II, propaganda was used by governments primarily to
choices: build public support, encourage enlistment, and shape attitudes toward the enemy
report objective news without bias
limit communication only to military officers
replace religious instruction in schools
questions.correctText: build public support, encourage enlistment, and shape attitudes toward the enemy

questions.prompt: Which statement best captures a long-term consequence of the Treaty of Versailles?
choices: It created economic and political resentments in Germany that fascist leaders later exploited
It produced lasting peace and prosperity throughout Europe
It eliminated all border disputes in Eastern Europe
It guaranteed full disarmament of every major power
questions.correctText: It created economic and political resentments in Germany that fascist leaders later exploited

questions.prompt: Which factor contributed most directly to the appeal of fascism in Italy and Germany after World War I?
choices: Economic hardship, political instability, and national humiliation
A long tradition of democratic political parties
Strong colonial empires already in place
The success of the League of Nations
questions.correctText: Economic hardship, political instability, and national humiliation

questions.prompt: The Great Depression contributed to the rise of dictators in the 1930s because
choices: mass unemployment and despair led many to support leaders promising radical change
global trade rose to record levels in Europe
democratic governments quickly solved economic problems
communism was outlawed in every country
questions.correctText: mass unemployment and despair led many to support leaders promising radical change

questions.prompt: Why did the League of Nations fail to prevent aggression in the 1930s?
choices: It lacked enforcement power and key members, including the United States, did not join
It commanded the largest army in the world
It successfully sanctioned Germany and Japan with armed force
It was dominated by the Soviet Union
questions.correctText: It lacked enforcement power and key members, including the United States, did not join

questions.prompt: Japan's withdrawal from the League of Nations in the 1930s reflected its
choices: rejection of international criticism of its actions in Manchuria
commitment to disarmament
support for Korean independence
alliance with the Soviet Union
questions.correctText: rejection of international criticism of its actions in Manchuria

questions.prompt: Stalin's Five-Year Plans were designed primarily to
choices: rapidly industrialize the Soviet Union through centralized state planning
create a free-market economy in the USSR
expand individual farms and private property
withdraw the Soviet Union from international trade entirely
questions.correctText: rapidly industrialize the Soviet Union through centralized state planning

questions.prompt: Soviet collectivization of agriculture under Stalin contributed to
choices: widespread famine, especially in Ukraine, during the early 1930s
rapid improvements in peasant living standards
a constitutional democracy in the countryside
the rise of independent labor unions
questions.correctText: widespread famine, especially in Ukraine, during the early 1930s

questions.prompt: The German remilitarization of the Rhineland in 1936 violated
choices: the Treaty of Versailles and the Locarno Pact
the Atlantic Charter
the Nuremberg Laws
the Nazi-Soviet Pact
questions.correctText: the Treaty of Versailles and the Locarno Pact

questions.prompt: The Anschluss of 1938 refers to
choices: Germany's annexation of Austria
the Italian invasion of Ethiopia
the Soviet attack on Finland
the Japanese conquest of Korea
questions.correctText: Germany's annexation of Austria

questions.prompt: By taking the Sudetenland in 1938, Hitler
choices: expanded German territory at Czechoslovakia's expense without fighting a major war
ended German military rearmament
established peace with the Soviet Union
renounced the Treaty of Versailles in writing
questions.correctText: expanded German territory at Czechoslovakia's expense without fighting a major war

questions.prompt: The phrase "lebensraum," or "living space," was used by Nazi leaders to justify
choices: German expansion into Eastern Europe at the expense of Slavic peoples
the colonization of Africa
an alliance with the Soviet Union
the protection of Germany's western borders
questions.correctText: German expansion into Eastern Europe at the expense of Slavic peoples

questions.prompt: An important goal of Japan's "Greater East Asia Co-Prosperity Sphere" was to
choices: mask Japanese imperial domination of much of Asia under the language of cooperation
create a free-trade zone modeled on the League of Nations
give independence to Korea and Taiwan
promote democracy throughout East Asia
questions.correctText: mask Japanese imperial domination of much of Asia under the language of cooperation

questions.prompt: Which best describes the experience of European Jews in Nazi-occupied territories during World War II?
choices: They were stripped of rights, confined to ghettos, deported, and murdered in camps
They were given autonomous self-government
They were largely shielded by international agreements
They were permitted to emigrate freely to any country
questions.correctText: They were stripped of rights, confined to ghettos, deported, and murdered in camps

questions.prompt: The Warsaw Ghetto Uprising of 1943 is significant because it
choices: represented armed Jewish resistance against Nazi deportations despite overwhelming odds
ended the German occupation of Poland
convinced the Allies to invade Poland from the west
freed millions of Jewish prisoners
questions.correctText: represented armed Jewish resistance against Nazi deportations despite overwhelming odds

questions.prompt: Allied strategic bombing of cities such as Dresden, Hamburg, and Tokyo aimed to
choices: destroy industrial capacity and demoralize Axis populations
spare civilian populations entirely
support Soviet ground advances only
create new colonies in Asia and Europe
questions.correctText: destroy industrial capacity and demoralize Axis populations

questions.prompt: During World War II, the home fronts in countries such as Britain and the United States were marked by
choices: rationing, war-bond drives, and the mobilization of civilians for industrial work
minimal involvement of civilians in the war effort
an end to all government regulation of the economy
a return to pre-industrial farming
questions.correctText: rationing, war-bond drives, and the mobilization of civilians for industrial work

questions.prompt: The Manhattan Project was
choices: the secret U.S.-led program to develop the atomic bomb during World War II
an Allied plan to invade Japan with conventional forces
the German effort to produce jet aircraft
a Soviet rocket program in the 1930s
questions.correctText: the secret U.S.-led program to develop the atomic bomb during World War II

questions.prompt: Which group of leaders made up the "Big Three" of the wartime Allied coalition?
choices: Franklin D. Roosevelt, Winston Churchill, and Joseph Stalin
Adolf Hitler, Benito Mussolini, and Hideki Tojo
Charles de Gaulle, Chiang Kai-shek, and Harry S. Truman
Neville Chamberlain, Léon Blum, and Stalin
questions.correctText: Franklin D. Roosevelt, Winston Churchill, and Joseph Stalin

questions.prompt: Which leader is most directly associated with rallying British morale during the early years of World War II?
choices: Winston Churchill
Neville Chamberlain
Stanley Baldwin
Clement Attlee
questions.correctText: Winston Churchill

questions.prompt: Charles de Gaulle is best known during World War II as
choices: the leader of the Free French forces opposing Nazi occupation
the head of the Vichy collaborationist government
the prime minister of Britain
the commander of Allied forces in the Pacific
questions.correctText: the leader of the Free French forces opposing Nazi occupation

questions.prompt: Which condition best describes life in Nazi-occupied Europe?
choices: Harsh repression, forced labor, deportations, and resistance movements in many countries
Generous self-rule for occupied peoples
Free elections under German supervision
Religious freedom for all citizens
questions.correctText: Harsh repression, forced labor, deportations, and resistance movements in many countries

questions.prompt: Resistance movements in occupied countries such as France, Poland, and Yugoslavia commonly
choices: sabotaged German operations and gathered intelligence for the Allies
openly collaborated with the Nazi regime
remained inactive throughout the war
fought primarily against the Allies
questions.correctText: sabotaged German operations and gathered intelligence for the Allies

questions.prompt: The 1939 Soviet attack on Finland (the "Winter War") demonstrated that
choices: Stalin's USSR was willing to expand militarily in addition to negotiating with Hitler
Finland was a member of the Axis powers
the League of Nations was effective in stopping aggression
the Soviet Union supported democracy in Scandinavia
questions.correctText: Stalin's USSR was willing to expand militarily in addition to negotiating with Hitler

questions.prompt: Which factor best explains why Germany's invasion of the Soviet Union failed to achieve its goals?
choices: Determined Soviet resistance, vast distances, and harsh winter conditions
Lack of any German air power
Soviet alliance with Japan
American troops fighting on the Eastern Front
questions.correctText: Determined Soviet resistance, vast distances, and harsh winter conditions

questions.prompt: The siege of Leningrad during World War II is remembered for
choices: the prolonged blockade by German forces that caused mass starvation among Soviet civilians
a quick German victory followed by a peace treaty
the surrender of Soviet forces in the city
the use of atomic weapons by the Soviets
questions.correctText: the prolonged blockade by German forces that caused mass starvation among Soviet civilians

questions.prompt: The German defeat at the Battle of Kursk in 1943
choices: ended Germany's ability to launch large offensives on the Eastern Front
halted Allied advances in Italy
ensured a Japanese victory in the Pacific
led to a separate peace between Germany and the USSR
questions.correctText: ended Germany's ability to launch large offensives on the Eastern Front

questions.prompt: The Doolittle Raid on Tokyo in April 1942
choices: showed that the Japanese home islands were vulnerable to American air attack
destroyed most of the Japanese navy
ended the war in the Pacific
halted the Japanese invasion of China
questions.correctText: showed that the Japanese home islands were vulnerable to American air attack

questions.prompt: The Bataan Death March in 1942 involved
choices: brutal Japanese treatment of American and Filipino prisoners of war in the Philippines
the relocation of Japanese citizens to internment camps in the U.S.
a peaceful evacuation of civilians from Manila
an Allied amphibious assault on the Japanese mainland
questions.correctText: brutal Japanese treatment of American and Filipino prisoners of war in the Philippines

questions.prompt: Which best describes the global scope of World War II?
choices: It involved fighting and devastation in Europe, Asia, Africa, the Pacific, and the Atlantic
It was limited entirely to Europe
It was fought only by colonial empires in Africa
It involved only naval engagements
questions.correctText: It involved fighting and devastation in Europe, Asia, Africa, the Pacific, and the Atlantic

questions.prompt: The Battle of the Atlantic primarily involved
choices: Allied convoys defending supply lines against German U-boats
land battles in coastal France
an Allied invasion of Spain
Japanese attacks on Atlantic shipping
questions.correctText: Allied convoys defending supply lines against German U-boats

questions.prompt: The Tehran Conference of 1943 brought together
choices: Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin to coordinate Allied strategy, including a second front in Western Europe
Hitler, Mussolini, and Tojo to plan a joint Axis offensive
the leaders of neutral states to negotiate peace
the founders of the United Nations
questions.correctText: Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin to coordinate Allied strategy, including a second front in Western Europe

questions.prompt: The unconditional surrender policy adopted by the Allies meant that
choices: the Axis powers would have to surrender without negotiating terms
Germany could keep its existing borders after the war
Japan would be allowed to retain its empire
individual Nazi leaders would not face trial
questions.correctText: the Axis powers would have to surrender without negotiating terms

questions.prompt: Which statement best explains why World War II is considered the deadliest conflict in human history?
choices: It caused tens of millions of military and civilian deaths, including those of the Holocaust
It was fought entirely with conventional weapons
It was concentrated in a single region of the world
It primarily affected only military personnel
questions.correctText: It caused tens of millions of military and civilian deaths, including those of the Holocaust

questions.prompt: After World War II, Germany and Berlin were each
choices: divided into four occupation zones controlled by the United States, Britain, France, and the Soviet Union
left under continued Nazi administration
annexed entirely by the Soviet Union
returned immediately to a single democratic government
questions.correctText: divided into four occupation zones controlled by the United States, Britain, France, and the Soviet Union

questions.prompt: U.S. General Douglas MacArthur played a major role in postwar Japan by
choices: overseeing the Allied occupation and a new democratic constitution for Japan
leading the invasion of Normandy
negotiating the Yalta agreement
commanding Soviet armies in the Pacific
questions.correctText: overseeing the Allied occupation and a new democratic constitution for Japan

questions.prompt: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) was a postwar effort to
choices: establish a common standard of fundamental rights for all peoples in the wake of WWII atrocities
create a military alliance against the Soviet Union
guarantee colonial powers continued control of their empires
abolish national governments
questions.correctText: establish a common standard of fundamental rights for all peoples in the wake of WWII atrocities

questions.prompt: The concept of "crimes against humanity," articulated at Nuremberg, established that
choices: individuals, including state officials, can be held legally responsible for atrocities
only nation-states, not individuals, can be punished after a war
wartime conduct is always legally protected
religious leaders bear no responsibility for state violence
questions.correctText: individuals, including state officials, can be held legally responsible for atrocities

questions.prompt: Which long-term effect on global power emerged from World War II?
choices: The decline of Western European empires and the rise of the U.S. and USSR as superpowers
The restoration of all European colonial empires to their pre-1939 status
The disappearance of nationalism throughout the world
The replacement of nation-states with the United Nations as the primary government
questions.correctText: The decline of Western European empires and the rise of the U.S. and USSR as superpowers

questions.prompt: Which generalization is best supported by the interwar period and World War II?
choices: Economic crisis, unresolved grievances, and weak international institutions can create conditions for renewed global conflict
Aggressive dictatorships are typically restrained by international goodwill alone
Trade barriers and tariffs tend to promote peaceful relations between major powers
Genocide is impossible in modern industrial societies
questions.correctText: Economic crisis, unresolved grievances, and weak international institutions can create conditions for renewed global conflict
